BandZoneRef.description:
One crossed-zone axis that is a numeric band ``[min, max)``.

``min``/``max`` are ``int`` for an integer-stepped axis (elevation feet) and
fractional for a bucketed one (the dimensionless ``[-1, 1]`` aspect components);
``unit`` is that axis' zone unit, or ``null`` when the measure is dimensionless.
BandZoneRef.title: BandZoneRef

BandedZoneInfo.description:
A banded zone axis: overridable band width over a covered numeric range.

``key`` is the value the ``zone`` query param accepts; the override query
param is ``'<key>.<param>'`` (likewise the other overridable kinds below).
BandedZoneInfo.title: BandedZoneInfo

BucketedZoneInfo.description: An even-bucketed dimensionless axis: overridable bucket count.
BucketedZoneInfo.title: BucketedZoneInfo

CategoricalZoneInfo.description: A categorical axis: fixed classes, no override param.
CategoricalZoneInfo.title: CategoricalZoneInfo

ClassZoneRef.description: One crossed-zone axis that is a discrete class (its ``code`` + ``label``).
ClassZoneRef.title: ClassZoneRef

CompactStatsResponse.description:
The compact zonal-stats envelope: pourpoint/query echo + the compact body.

Unlike :class:`StatsResponse`, this is a single generic model shared by every
dataset — the compact body carries no per-dataset field names — so it is not
parametrized per dataset.
CompactStatsResponse.title: CompactStatsResponse

CompactZone.description:
One crossed-zone cell in the compact form: its refs plus hoisted area.

``area_m2`` is date-invariant (a property of the crossed-zone geometry), so it
is stated once here rather than repeated per date as the verbose per-date model
does.
CompactZone.title: CompactZone

Coverage.description:
How fully a dataset's grid contains a pourpoint's basin geometry.

``FULL`` -- the grid extent covers the whole basin (the only state a zonal
query may run over without clipping); ``PARTIAL`` -- the basin overlaps the
grid but spills outside it (a query would silently use only the in-grid
portion); ``NONE`` -- the basin is entirely outside the grid (an empty mask).
Coverage.title: Coverage

DatasetListItem.description:
One entry in the dataset list: its name plus a ``self`` link to its detail
route, so each item is a followable resource rather than a bare string paired
with a link stranded in the collection's ``links`` array.
DatasetListItem.title: DatasetListItem

DatetimeInterval.description:
An RFC 3339 instant or interval, as accepted by the OGC ``datetime`` param.

``start``/``end`` of ``None`` denote an open (unbounded) end. An instant
(a single timestamp with no ``/``) is represented as ``start == end``.
DatetimeInterval.title: DatetimeInterval

GridInfo.description: A summary of a dataset's tiled grid.
GridInfo.title: GridInfo

mean_albedo_dirty_terrain_corrected_perc.title: Mean Albedo Dirty Terrain Corrected Percent

mean_radiative_forcing_w_per_m2.title: Mean Radiative Forcing W Per M2

Link.description: An OGC-style link. Null fields are omitted on JSON serialization.
Link.title: Link

PourpointDetailProperties.description: The single-record (detail) properties: the summary set + curated ids.

examples: Clark Fork R at St. Regis
name.title: Name

PourpointDetailProperties.title: PourpointDetailProperties

PourpointProperties.description:
A pourpoint's summary (list) properties -- the denormalized index fields.

Fixed regardless of the ``geometry`` view: only the feature ``geometry`` slot
changes between point and basin mode, never these properties. ``pourpoint`` is
the outflow ``[lon, lat]`` and is always present (free from the index), so the
basin view still carries the point.

examples: Clark Fork R at St. Regis
name.title: Name

PourpointProperties.title: PourpointProperties

ProblemType.description:
A documented, reusable kind of problem: a stable ``type`` URI plus defaults.

Define these once and raise them by reference, so a service's error catalog lives
in one place and its ``type`` URIs stop defaulting to ``about:blank`` and stay
stable/linkable. The per-occurrence ``detail``/``instance`` (and any extension
members) are supplied at the raise site::

    NOT_FOUND = ProblemType(
        type='https://errors.example/not-found', title='Resource not found', status=404,
    )
    raise NOT_FOUND.exception(detail='plant 5 not found', instance='/plants/5')
ProblemType.title: ProblemType

mean_precip_liquid_kg_per_m2.title: Mean Precip Liquid Kg Per M2

mean_precip_solid_kg_per_m2.title: Mean Precip Solid Kg Per M2

ThresholdZoneInfo.description: A threshold-split axis: overridable split point within its measured range.
ThresholdZoneInfo.title: ThresholdZoneInfo

ThresholdZoneRef.description:
One crossed-zone axis that is a threshold split (forested vs unforested).

``threshold`` (in ``unit``) is the split point as a real value; ``side`` is
``'below'`` or ``'above'`` (at-or-above); ``label`` is the human name of the
side (e.g. ``'forested'``).
ThresholdZoneRef.title: ThresholdZoneRef

VariableInfo.description: A requestable variable of a dataset and how its stat is reported.
VariableInfo.title: VariableInfo

ZoneClassInfo.description: One categorical class a zone layer stratifies into (key + human label).
ZoneClassInfo.title: ZoneClassInfo

_StatsFormat.description: The requested output format, as one of the supported representation keys. Takes precedence over the `Accept` header; an unsupported value returns a `400` problem. Supported keys: `json`, `csv`.
_StatsFormat.title: _StatsFormat

info.description: Pourpoint metadata and snow zonal statistics.
info.title: PSU CSAR snowtool API

parameters.description: Stratify by a zone layer (repeatable).
parameters.name: zone

schema.description: Stratify by a zone layer (repeatable).
schema.title: Zone

parameters.description: Variable to report (repeatable; default all).
parameters.name: variable

schema.description: Variable to report (repeatable; default all).
schema.title: Variable

parameters.description: Permit a basin only partially covered by the dataset grid. By default a partially-covered basin is a 409 (guarding against stats silently computed over just the covered slice but reported as the whole basin); set true to accept a knowingly-clipped query over the covered portion. A wholly off-grid basin always 409s regardless.
parameters.name: allow_partial

schema.description: Permit a basin only partially covered by the dataset grid. By default a partially-covered basin is a 409 (guarding against stats silently computed over just the covered slice but reported as the whole basin); set true to accept a knowingly-clipped query over the covered portion. A wholly off-grid basin always 409s regardless.
schema.title: Allow Partial

parameters.description: Include crossed zones that no AOI pixel falls in (0 area, all stats null). By default these empty combinations are dropped, which trims a response that would otherwise grow combinatorically with the number of crossed zone axes; set true to emit the full zone product. No effect on a whole-basin (unstratified) query.
parameters.name: include_empty_zones

schema.description: Include crossed zones that no AOI pixel falls in (0 area, all stats null). By default these empty combinations are dropped, which trims a response that would otherwise grow combinatorically with the number of crossed zone axes; set true to emit the full zone product. No effect on a whole-basin (unstratified) query.
schema.title: Include Empty Zones

parameters.description: Stratify by a zone layer (repeatable): LAYER or LAYER:PARAM=VALUE. See GET /datasets/{dataset} for the valid layer keys, override params, and variables. Default: whole basin.
parameters.name: zone

schema.description: Stratify by a zone layer (repeatable): LAYER or LAYER:PARAM=VALUE. See GET /datasets/{dataset} for the valid layer keys, override params, and variables. Default: whole basin.
schema.title: Zone

parameters.description: Variable to report (repeatable; default: all). Use a variable key from GET /datasets/{dataset}.
parameters.name: variable

schema.description: Variable to report (repeatable; default: all). Use a variable key from GET /datasets/{dataset}.
schema.title: Variable

parameters.description: Permit a basin only partially covered by the dataset grid (default false: a partially-covered basin is a 409). A wholly off-grid basin always 409s.
parameters.name: allow_partial

schema.description: Permit a basin only partially covered by the dataset grid (default false: a partially-covered basin is a 409). A wholly off-grid basin always 409s.
schema.title: Allow Partial

parameters.description: Include crossed zones that no AOI pixel falls in (0 area, all values null). By default these empty combinations are dropped. No effect on a whole-basin query.
parameters.name: include_empty_zones

schema.description: Include crossed zones that no AOI pixel falls in (0 area, all values null). By default these empty combinations are dropped. No effect on a whole-basin query.
schema.title: Include Empty Zones

parameters.description: An RFC 3339 instant, or a `start/end` interval where either side may be open using `..` (or empty). An instant matches a single timestamp; an interval matches a range. Malformed values return a `400` problem.
parameters.name: datetime

schema.description: An RFC 3339 instant, or a `start/end` interval where either side may be open using `..` (or empty). An instant matches a single timestamp; an interval matches a range. Malformed values return a `400` problem.
schema.title: Datetime

parameters.description: A bounding box, as comma-separated numbers `minx,miny,maxx,maxy` (WGS 84 longitude/latitude), or `minx,miny,minz,maxx,maxy,maxz` for a 3D box. The x axis may wrap the antimeridian (`minx` may exceed `maxx`); the y (and z) axes must be ordered `min <= max`. Malformed values return a `400` problem.
parameters.name: bbox

schema.description: A bounding box, as comma-separated numbers `minx,miny,maxx,maxy` (WGS 84 longitude/latitude), or `minx,miny,minz,maxx,maxy,maxz` for a 3D box. The x axis may wrap the antimeridian (`minx` may exceed `maxx`); the y (and z) axes must be ordered `min <= max`. Malformed values return a `400` problem.
schema.title: Bbox

get.description:
The catalog of problem types this API raises, keyed by short name.

Each problem ``type`` URI resolves to its entry here, so a client can look up
the meaning of a received ``application/problem+json`` ``type``.
